annotations.label: Geometric group theory

anchor.snippet: Geometric group theory is an area in mathematics devoted to the study of finitely generated groups

annotations.note: The field itself is not formalized, but its central object—finitely generated groups—is captured by the `Group.FG` class.

annotations.label: Cayley graph and word metric

anchor.snippet: studying the Cayley graphs of groups

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.mulCayley` directly defines the Cayley graph from a generating set, though the word metric is not separately packaged.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic group

anchor.snippet: introduced the notion of a hyperbolic group

annotations.note: Mathlib's only 'hyperbolic' references are to hyperbolic functions/geometry, not Gromov-hyperbolic groups.

annotations.label: Quasi-isometric classification of groups

anchor.snippet: classifying finitely generated groups with their word metric up to quasi-isometry

annotations.note: No notion of quasi-isometry appears anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gromov's polynomial growth theorem
annotations.note: No formalization of group growth or Gromov's theorem exists (the only 'polynomial growth' is Akra–Bazzi function asymptotics).

annotations.label: Stallings' ends theorem
annotations.note: Neither the ends of groups nor Stallings' theorem is formalized.

annotations.label: Mostow rigidity theorem
annotations.note: Mostow rigidity is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relatively hyperbolic groups

anchor.snippet: The notion of a relatively hyperbolic groups was originally introduced by Gromov

annotations.note: Relatively hyperbolic groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Automatic groups

anchor.snippet: the development of the theory of automatic groups

annotations.note: Automatic groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Amenable groups
annotations.note: Mathlib has Følner-filter amenability (`IsFoelner.amenable`) but, per its own docstring, no standalone definition of an amenable group.

annotations.label: Free Burnside groups
annotations.note: Mathlib's 'Burnside' refers to Burnside's lemma/normal complement theorem, not the free Burnside groups B(m,n).

annotations.label: Infinite cyclic group Z
annotations.note: The infinite cyclic group is exactly ℤ, whose additive group structure is `Int.instAddCommGroup`.

annotations.label: Free groups
annotations.note: `FreeGroup α` is the free group on a generating type α.

annotations.label: Free products
annotations.note: `Monoid.CoprodI` is the free product (coproduct) of a family of groups, with binary `Monoid.Coprod` as a special case.

annotations.label: Outer automorphism groups Out(Fn)

anchor.snippet: Outer automorphism groups Out(F n ) (via outer space )

annotations.note: Mathlib has `MulAut` and `FreeGroup` but no outer automorphism group (Aut/Inn) construction.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic groups
annotations.note: Gromov-hyperbolic groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mapping class groups
annotations.note: Mapping class groups of surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetric groups
annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α` is the symmetric group of permutations of α.

annotations.label: Braid groups
annotations.note: Mathlib's 'braid' references are to braided monoidal categories, not the braid groups.

annotations.label: Coxeter groups
annotations.note: `CoxeterSystem` and the `IsCoxeterGroup` class formalize Coxeter groups.

annotations.label: General Artin groups
annotations.note: Artin groups are not defined; Mathlib only mentions the 'Artin–Tits relation' in a comment within the Coxeter files.

annotations.label: Thompson's group F
annotations.note: Thompson's group F is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CAT(0) groups
annotations.note: CAT(0) spaces/groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arithmetic groups
annotations.note: There is no notion of arithmetic group in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Automatic groups
annotations.note: Automatic groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fuchsian and Kleinian groups

anchor.snippet: Fuchsian groups , Kleinian groups , and other groups acting properly discontinuously on symmetric spaces

annotations.note: Neither Fuchsian nor Kleinian groups are formalized.

annotations.label: Wallpaper groups
annotations.note: Wallpaper (crystallographic) groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Baumslag–Solitar groups
annotations.note: Baumslag–Solitar groups are HNN extensions of ℤ; Mathlib has the general `HNNExtension` construction but not these specific groups.

annotations.label: Fundamental groups of graphs of groups

anchor.snippet: Fundamental groups of graphs of groups

annotations.note: The Bass–Serre building blocks—amalgamated products (`Monoid.PushoutI`) and HNN extensions—exist, but graph-of-groups fundamental groups are not formalized.

annotations.label: Grigorchuk group
annotations.note: The Grigorchuk group is not formalized in Mathlib.
